name: Virtual Civilian Infinite Scaling (Synthetic Ascension)
description: An exponentially scaling build that abuses the Virtual Computational Core Focus policy. Every 100 civilians provides +0.5% to research/unity/alloys. With proper setup, you can reach millions of research per month through unlimited civilian pop scaling. The build transforms from biological → synthetic → virtual, then switches to Megacorp to maximize civilian production with Corporate Hedonism. Requires Machine Age DLC.

strategy.core_mechanic: Computational Core Focus policy (hidden/unnamed in game) - Every 100 civilians = +0.5% output to chosen resource. With Corporate Hedonism (Megacorp civic), civilians produce trade/unity and scale infinitely without job limits.
strategy.early_game: Start with Synthetic Fertility origin. Rush engineering research to unlock Artificial Workforce tech. Build Engineering Research Specialization district for massive engineering output. Use Digital Salvation situation to convert biological pops into Identity Repository while unlocking robot assembly.
strategy.mid_game: Complete Synthetic Ascension tradition tree. Transform all pops to synthetic. CRITICAL: During Virtual transformation event chain, choose options that lead to 'fake virtual' state (Virtual + Corporate authority). Key choice: 'Research Turning Into Profits' option during transformation.
strategy.government_reform: After synthetic ascension, reform government to Megacorp authority. Replace Parliamentary System with Corporate Hedonism civic. This gives Heatedness jobs that produce unity and don't use job slots - allowing unlimited civilian scaling.
strategy.late_game: Enable 'Computational Core Focus' policy and set to Research. Build Ecumenopolises with maximum city districts. Every 100 civilians = +0.5% research. Build Robot Quantum Production Hubs (empire unique building, 300 roboticist jobs each) on every planet to maximize pop assembly. At 1.3 million pops with 71,000+ civilians, achieve 570%+ research bonus.
strategy.key_buildings: Robot Quantum Production Hubs (empire unique, requires Cosmogenesis), Identity Complexes for assembly speed, Research Labs on Ecumenopolis
strategy.why_broken: Civilians scale exponentially because: 1) They provide assembly speed (more pops = faster growth), 2) Each 100 civilians = +0.5% research, 3) More research = faster tech = better assembly, 4) Corporate Hedonism civilians don't use job slots = infinite stacking possible. The policy has no cap and wasn't properly named/balanced by devs.

difficulty: Very Complex - requires precise event choices during Virtual transformation
notes: Build discovered by community member 'Scope' and shared via Stellaris Discord. The Computational Core Focus policy literally has no proper localization text in-game, showing devs may have overlooked this mechanic. Can reach 1+ million research per month in late game.
